articles.title: Trump says US strikes hit Iran in 'honour' of American soldiers killed
articles.summary: Iran responded with attacks in Syria and Jordan, as two ships reportedly exploded in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

articles.title: Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify
articles.summary: Russian strikes on the Odesa region have killed 28 people in July alone, local authorities say.

articles.summary: The parents of the four naturally-conceived girls were taken by "immense surprise" with the pregnancy, the hospital said.

articles.title: Notorious drug cartel leader Ismael Zambada sentenced to life in US prison
articles.summary: Known as "El Mayo", the co-founder the Sinaloa Cartel was also ordered to pay $15bn after he admitted to sending vast amounts of drugs to the US.

articles.title: Boy, 13, dies during World Cup celebrations in Spain

articles.title: Police block thousands of 'cockroach' protesters marching on India's parliament
articles.summary: Some protesters demanding education reforms said they were injured in clashes with police who banned the march.

articles.title: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader
articles.summary: The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.

articles.title: Scores feared dead after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana
articles.summary: Sixty-seven people have been rescued but the ferry is thought to have had more than 130 people on board.

articles.title: Newcastle, Liverpool and England legend Keegan dies
articles.summary: Former England captain and manager Kevin Keegan dies at the age of 75, seven weeks after he revealed he had been diagnosed with stage four cancer.
